mechanism_narrative: Rising federal funds rates increase borrowing costs for corporations and reduce the present value of future equity cash flows, generating negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Rank IC exceeds 0.05 in the positive direction over a 3-year rolling window, or forward returns are uncorrelated with rate changes.

mechanism_narrative: When the unemployment rate exceeds 5.5 percent, labor market slack signals weakening consumer demand and corporate earnings deterioration, driving negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: Forward returns remain positive in >65% of triggered instances, or the signal loses predictive power when unemployment stays below 5.5 percent.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous increases in existing home sales and housing starts indicate robust housing sector momentum, which amplifies consumer spending and financial sector leverage, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over 20 trading days.
failure_condition: The joint signal fails to outperform a single-series baseline, or forward returns turn negative when both series rise concurrently over a 2-year sample.

mechanism_narrative: In a late-cycle expansion regime, rising industrial production reflects peak capacity utilization and strong corporate output, which translates to positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: The signal reverses direction or becomes statistically insignificant when applied to early-cycle or recession regimes, or Rank IC drops below 0.02 in late-cycle windows.

mechanism_narrative: Growth in total nonfarm payrolls directly expands household income and aggregate consumption, supporting corporate revenue growth and generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Payroll growth shows zero or negative correlation with 20-day forward returns across multiple rolling windows, or the signal is subsumed by monetary policy variables.

mechanism_narrative: When monthly inflation exceeds 0.4 percent, anticipated central bank tightening and margin compression trigger risk-off positioning, causing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: Equity returns remain positive or neutral during high-inflation months, or the threshold fails to predict direction in >70% of triggered cases over a 3-year horizon.

mechanism_narrative: Divergence between rising GDP growth and rising unemployment indicates inefficient job creation and structural economic weakness, which suppresses equity valuations and yields negative S&P 500 forward returns over 60 trading days.
failure_condition: The divergence signal shows no predictive edge over aligned GDP and unemployment trends, or forward returns are positive when both series move in opposite directions.

mechanism_narrative: When existing home sales fall below 500,000 units, housing market distress signals broader consumer deleveraging and reduced wealth effects, driving negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: Forward returns are positive in >60% of instances when sales drop below the threshold, or the signal loses statistical significance in out-of-sample testing.
